title: National government administrative occupations
description: Determine and formulate policies and provide overall direction of companies or private and public sector organizations within guidelines set up by a board of directors or similar governing body. Plan, direct, or coordinate operational activities at the highest level of management with the help of subordinate executives and staff managers. Communicate and coordinate with management, shareholders, customers, and employees to address sustainability issues. Enact or oversee a corporate sustainability strategy. Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. (Based on 46 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Issue public notification of all official activities or meetings.
Perform administrative tasks, such as answering telephone calls, filing court documents, or maintaining office supplies or equipment.
Record and edit the minutes of meetings and distribute to appropriate officials or staff members.
Record case dispositions, court orders, or arrangements made for payment of court fees.
Prepare meeting agendas or packets of related information, including ordinances and resolutions for execution.
Research information in the municipal archives upon request of public officials or private citizens.
Verify the authenticity of documents, such as foreign identification or immigration documents, and examine legal documents for adherence to laws or procedures.
Respond to requests for information from the public, municipalities, or state officials regarding licensing policies, judicial procedures, or court appearances.
Negotiate, administer, and monitor contracts with suppliers, vendors, and other representatives to ensure compliance and performance.
Develop and maintain liaisons with municipalities and similar entities to facilitate plan development and response coordination.
Prepare plans outlining emergency procedures for response and recovery from disasters, and ensure adherence to regulations.
Monitor changes in laws and regulations impacting emergency plans and ensure that they remain compliant with central and devolved government guidelines.
Provide information and guidance to citizens about community services and recommend options to facilitate problem resolution.
Analyze price proposals and conduct market research to inform purchasing strategies and decisions.
Perform budgeting duties, assisting in budget preparation and review of expenditures.
Conduct or coordinate training for staff or the community on emergency preparedness and management practices.
Direct, plan, or implement policies, objectives, or activities of organizations to ensure operations and maximize returns on investments.
Develop, implement, and revise departmental policies and procedures to enhance organizational effectiveness.
Review and analyze legislation, laws, or public policy and recommend changes to support the interests of the general population.
Preside over, or serve on, boards of directors, management committees, or other governing boards to oversee organizational direction.
Confer with board members, organization officials, or staff members to discuss issues, coordinate activities, or resolve problems.
Prepare or present reports concerning activities, expenses, budgets, or government statutes that affect program services.
Meet with civic, educational, and community groups to develop programs and discuss policies related to sustainability efforts.
Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of sustainability programs to ensure compliance with policies, standards, and regulations.
Research environmental sustainability issues and stakeholder interests to inform organizational policy and strategy.
Develop sustainability reports and proposals for various stakeholders to promote environmental initiatives.
Maintain or update business intelligence tools, databases, dashboards, systems, or methods.
Communicate with customers, competitors, suppliers, and others to stay abreast of industry or business trends.
Prepare and process import and export documentation according to customs regulations, laws, or procedures.
Advise customers on import and export restrictions, tariff systems, insurance requirements, quotas, or other customs-related matters.
Confer with officials in various agencies to facilitate clearance of goods through customs and quarantine.
Prepare reports of activities, evaluations, recommendations, or decisions.
Evaluate applications, records, or documents to gather information about eligibility or liability issues.
Inspect government property to ensure compliance with contract specifications or legal requirements.
Prepare or maintain technical files as necessary to obtain and sustain product approval.
Coordinate efforts associated with the preparation of regulatory documents or submissions.
Determine which sites and violation reports to investigate, and coordinate compliance and enforcement activities with government agencies.
Research and stay informed of developments in EPA laws and regulations.
Compile reports, charts, or graphs that describe and interpret findings of analyses and prepare documentation needed for investigations.
Coordinate the release of personal effects to authorized persons and facilitate the disposition of unclaimed corpses.
Confer with officials of public health and law enforcement agencies to coordinate interdepartmental activities.
Provide information concerning the circumstances of death to relatives of the deceased, including details required for case finalization.
Perform medicolegal examinations and, when necessary, conduct preliminary examinations of bodies to identify victims and signs of trauma.
Interview and investigate applicants for public assistance to gather information pertinent to their applications.
Compile, record, and evaluate personal and financial data to determine eligibility status for assistance programs.
Respond to questions about benefits and claim procedures while interpreting eligibility requirements and legal rights to applicants.
Review complaints, petitions, or motions to determine issues involved, and provide recommendations when necessary.
Investigation of facts and law of cases, and search pertinent public records and sources to prepare cases.
Prepare, edit, or review legal documents such as briefs, pleadings, or contracts for accuracy and compliance with regulations.
Confer with stakeholders including realtors and lending institutions to exchange title-related information and resolve issues.
Assess the legal status of land-related documents and verify the accuracy and completeness of registrations.
Prepare affidavits or other legal correspondence, organizing and maintaining documents in electronic and paper filing systems.
Meet with clients and other professionals to discuss the details of cases or to coordinate activities related to investigations or operations.
Prepare agendas and make arrangements for meetings, including coordinating catering and recording minutes.
Manage and maintain executives' schedules and complete work schedules, managing calendars and arranging appointments as needed.
Provide transcripts of proceedings upon request and manage records related to court cases and meetings.
Review operating practices to identify improvements in areas such as workflow and reporting procedures.
Compile, copy, sort, and file records of office activities, transactions, and maintain filing systems.
Communicate with customers and employees to answer questions and disseminate information.
Prepare, maintain, and review purchasing files, reports, and ensure accuracy of documents submitted.
Perform general office duties such as ordering supplies, maintaining records, and basic bookkeeping.
Conduct research, compile data, and prepare papers for consideration by executives or committees.
File and retrieve corporate documents, maintain inventory of materials and ensure correspondence is processed.
Inform customers of procedures for applying for services, such as ATM cards and direct deposit of checks.
Compile information about new accounts, enter account details into computers, and file related documents.
Provide consultative services in areas such as equipment evaluation, plant layout, or food safety systems.
Compare prices, specifications, and delivery dates to determine the best bid among potential suppliers.
Track the status of requisitions, contracts, and orders to ensure timely processing.
Calculate costs of orders and manage invoice processing to appropriate accounts.
Check shipments on arrival to ensure that orders are filled accurately and goods meet specifications.
Compare suppliers' bills with bids and purchase orders for accuracy verification.
Maintain knowledge of organizational and governmental purchasing rules and inform staff and vendors accordingly.
Monitor contractor performance and recommend contract modifications as necessary.
Inspect agricultural commodities or operations for compliance with health, quality, and safety regulations.
Inspect food products and processing procedures to determine safety standards.
Interpret and enforce government acts and regulations while explaining required standards to agricultural workers.
Review customer accounts to ensure timely payments and compliance with loan terms.
Establish or implement departmental policies, goals, objectives, or procedures in conjunction with management.
Direct or coordinate financial activities to fund operations and increase efficiency.
Monitor suppliers to ensure efficient provision of goods or services within budgetary limits.
Plan or direct operational activities that require coordination with department managers.
Review financial statements and reports to measure productivity and identify areas for improvement.
Perform personnel functions, such as selection and training, to enhance workforce capabilities.
